reviews.review_msg: INDG101, not 422. The course was relatively straightforward, with clear rubric outlines in the syllabus for everything. The biweekly quizzes were difficult so definitely review all of the weeks material. TA's have fast responses and detailed feedback when asked for further explanations or for tips before handing in assignments.

Yes the prof is a tough marker, but the guidelines and rubrics are really clear. If you don't leave things to the last minute and check your work against the rubric, you should do well. It's clear the prof is highly knowledgeable.

Course reused old materials from a different professor and often had mistakes to the syllabus and quizzes that had to be corrected. The materials included Indigenous perspectives, but was very Westernized it it's delivery. Very hard marker.

Prof was good overall in terms of lectures and giving rubrics for assignments, but were so condense in detail. Super hard grader with APA 7 citations and spelling/errors and seems to have specific things shes looking for in discussions. INDG classes are usually open-minded in terms of learning, but this one leans towards strict western learning.

Her class content is well informed and enjoyable but I recommend auditing her course, she is a very tough grader and very focused on citations and formatting. I understand sticking to assignment deadlines and late penalties are a part of academia but I also believe that academia wasn't built in a way that works for everyone.
